nodes.title: SCHEDULE C Prior Deferral Plan Benefit Payment Appendix
nodes.summary:
# Schedule C: Prior Deferral Plan Benefit Payment Appendix - Main Points

This Schedule C document outlines the payment timing rules for deferred compensation benefits from plans prior to 2011 that have been consolidated into the current Plan. Key points include:

## Overview
- Applies to deferral plan benefits from years prior to 2011 under earlier company-maintained plans
- Governed by Section 409A of the Internal Revenue Code
- Payment dates are approximate, with flexibility of up to 90 days from identified dates
- Administrator may update for administrative purposes without altering benefit payment timing or form

## Pre-2008 Deferral Plans
- Principal earned and vested through December 31, 2004 is "grandfathered" and exempt from Section 409A
- Grandfathered status applies to interest prior to 1999 and certain 1999-2004 interest for retirement-eligible participants
- Grandfathered status primarily impacts employees receiving severance payments
- Payment rules vary based on whether participant is "Retirement Eligible" (age 55+ with 10+ years of service) at termination
- Payments made on March 15 or September 15 dates, either as lump sums or installments
- Special rules apply for disability and death

## 2008-2010 Plans
- No grandfathered amounts; fully subject to Section 409A
- Similar payment structure to pre-2008 plans but with key differences in timing
- Severance status does not affect payment timing
- Payment commencement does not delay until specified dates if separation occurs early and participant is retirement eligible

## Degrandfathered Benefits
- Special transition rules for benefits that lost grandfathered status
- Default lump sum payment in March 2012 if no election made
- Specified dates could not be earlier than July 2017
- Payments made on January 1 or July 1 dates

nodes.title: Pre-2008 Deferral Plan Benefits
nodes.summary:
# Schedule C: Prior Deferral Plan Benefit Payment Appendix - Main Points

## Overview
- Details payment timing rules for Deferral Plan benefits from years prior to 2011 under earlier consolidated plans
- Governed by Section 1.3 of the Plan and Section 409A of the Code
- Payment dates are approximate, with flexibility of up to 90 days per Treasury Regulations
- Administrator may update for administrative purposes without altering benefit payment timing or form

## Pre-2008 Deferral Plans
- Principal earned and vested through December 31, 2004 is "grandfathered" and exempt from Section 409A
- Interest prior to 1999 and interest from 1999-2004 for Retirement Eligible participants are grandfathered
- Grandfathered status primarily impacts employees receiving severance payments
- "Retirement Eligible" defined as age 55+ with 10+ years of service

## Payment Rules by Scenario (Pre-2008 Plans)
- **Specified date elections**: Paid on specified date if still active; if terminated before date and not Retirement Eligible, paid in lump sum within 6 months
- **Retirement + X years elections**: Paid according to election timing; Retirement Eligible participants paid per election even if after termination
- **Retirement elections**: Paid per election if Retirement Eligible at termination; otherwise lump sum within 6 months
- **Disability**: Paid per election within 6 months of disability date
- **Death**: Account balance paid to beneficiary in lump sum within 6 months of death

## 2008-2010 Plans
- No grandfathered amounts; fully subject to Section 409A
- Rules set forth in Article 8 of Plan document as "Pre-2011 Deferral Benefits"
- Similar payment structure with Separation from Service requirements

nodes.title: 2008-2010 Plans
nodes.summary:
# Description of Partial Document

This document outlines the payment distribution rules for deferred compensation plans, specifically covering two main plan categories:

## Pre-2008 Plans (with Grandfathered Amounts)
- **Retirement scenarios**: Payments begin on the first March 15/September 15 at least 6 months after retirement, with options for lump sum or installments (subsequent installments paid each March 15)
- **Non-retirement termination**: Lump sum payment on the first March 15/September 15 at least 6 months following termination
- **Severance impact**: May delay payment start for grandfathered amounts based on deemed termination of employment
- **Disability**: Payments follow participant elections with the same 6-month minimum waiting period
- **Death**: Account balance paid as lump sum to designated beneficiary on the first March 15/September 15 at least 6 months after death

## 2008-2010 Plans (Section 409A Compliant, No Grandfathered Amounts)
- **Specified date elections**: Payment timing depends on whether separation from service occurs before or after the elected date
- **Retirement eligibility**: Determines whether installments continue or convert to lump sum
- **Non-retirement separation**: Results in lump sum payment at least 6 months following termination
- **Retirement-eligible separation**: Payments follow elected form (lump sum or installments) beginning at least 6 months after separation
- **Severance**: Does not affect or delay benefit payments since no amounts are grandfathered
- **Death/Disability**: Standard Pre-2011 Deferral Benefits rules apply

Key theme: All payments are subject to a minimum 6-month waiting period and are distributed on March 15 or September 15 payment dates.

nodes.title: Special Rule for Degrandfathered Participants' Degrandfathered Benefits Under Section 1.3 of the Plan
nodes.summary:
# Description of Partial Document

This section outlines special rules governing the distribution of degrandfathered benefits under Section 1.3 of a deferred compensation plan. The main points covered include:

1. **Scope**: Rules apply only to degrandfathered principal; interest is excluded and follows Pre-2008 Deferral Plan benefit rules.

2. **No Election Scenario**: Participants who did not make an election regarding degrandfathered benefits receive a lump sum payment in March 2012.

3. **Specified Date Election (Before Separation)**: 
   - Earliest specified date is July 2017
   - Lump sum payments occur on the specified January 1 or July 1 date
   - Installment payments begin on the specified date and continue semi-annually thereafter
   - Separation from Service after the specified date does not affect payment schedule

4. **Separation Before Specified Date or Retirement Election**:
   - Lump sum: Paid on the later of July 1, 2017 or the first January 1/July 1 at least six months after separation
   - Installments: First payment on the later of July 1, 2017 or the July 1 following separation year; subsequent payments on each July 1 thereafter

5. **Death Benefit**: Standard Post-2010 Deferral Benefits death benefit rules apply to deceased participants.

nodes.title: SCHEDULE D
nodes.summary:
# Schedule D - Plan Termination Description

This Schedule D outlines the irrevocable termination of a deferred compensation plan effective upon the closing of a transaction between American Express Travel Related Services Company, Inc. and a Time Inc. subsidiary. The main points covered include:

1. **Scope of Termination**: The plan termination applies only to employees of American Express Publishing Corporation (referred to as "Publishing Participants") as of the termination date.

2. **Cessation of Contributions**: All crediting of plan accounts for Publishing Participants ceases as of the termination date.

3. **Benefit Payment Requirements**: Remaining accrued and unpaid benefits for each Publishing Participant must be paid in accordance with Treasury Regulation Section 1.409A-3(j)(4)(ix)(B) within twelve months of the termination date.

4. **Benefit Determination**: Benefits are calculated according to the plan's existing provisions.

5. **Tax Compliance**: All benefit payments must comply with Section 409A of the Internal Revenue Code of 1986, as amended, ensuring proper tax treatment of deferred compensation distributions.

nodes.title: SCHEDULE E American Express Global Business Travel Divestiture
nodes.summary:
# Schedule E: American Express Global Business Travel Divestiture - Main Points

## Overview
This Schedule E governs plan participation for employees of the proposed Global Business Travel joint venture who are entitled to benefits comparable to those offered by American Express.

## Key Coverage Areas

**Continued Participation (Article 1)**
- Business Employees may continue participating during the period they receive comparable benefits
- Eligibility requires prior Plan participation or enrollment opportunity before the Transition Date
- Plan maintains "top-hat" status for ERISA purposes (limited to management/highly compensated employees)
- Participation terminates upon Separation from Service or when comparable benefits period ends

**Contributions (Article 2)**
- Deferral Benefits deducted from Base Salary and Incentive Pay per employee elections
- Supplemental Benefits may be allocated at American Express's discretion
- Prior Deferral Elections remain unaffected by Business Employee status
- Benefits remain unfunded obligations of the Company

**Vesting (Article 3)**
- Business employment treated as Company employment for vesting purposes
- Company contributions follow the Business's 401(k) plan vesting schedule without acceleration

**Earnings (Article 4)**
- Account credits follow standard Plan provisions

**Payment of Benefits (Article 5)**
- Payment timing and form determined as if employment were with the Company
- Separation from Service evaluated using the Business as the service recipient
- Beneficiary designations unaffected by Business Employee status

**Administrative Provisions (Article 6)**
- Committee retains amendment/termination rights
- Schedule compliance with Section 409A requirements maintained throughout

nodes.title: ARTICLE 1 CONTINUED PARTICIPATION
nodes.summary:
# Schedule E: American Express Global Business Travel Divestiture - Main Points

## Overview
This Schedule E governs plan participation for employees of the proposed Global Business Travel joint venture who are entitled to benefits comparable to those offered by American Express.

## Key Coverage Areas

**Continued Participation (Article 1)**
- Business Employees may continue participating during the period they receive comparable benefits
- Eligibility requires prior Plan participation or enrollment opportunity before the Transition Date
- Administrator has discretion to exclude participants if compliant with Section 409A
- Plan maintains "top-hat" status for ERISA purposes (limited to management/highly compensated employees)
- Participation terminates upon separation from service or when comparable benefits period ends
- Employees who return to American Express cease participation under this Schedule but retain accrued benefits

**Contributions (Article 2)**
- Deferral Benefits are deducted from Base Salary and Incentive Pay per the Business Employee's election
- Base Salary and Incentive Pay definitions align with amounts paid by the Business corresponding to Company standards
- Compensation from both Company and Business is aggregated for Section 401(a)(17) Limitation purposes
- Prior Deferral Elections remain unaffected by status change
- Supplemental Benefits may be allocated at American Express's discretion, considering Business payments
- No duplicative benefits are permitted

**Conflict Resolution**
- Schedule E controls over other Plan provisions for Business Employees, except regarding timing, form, and amount of pre-Schedule benefits

nodes.title: Section 1.1 Eligibility to Participate
nodes.summary:
# Description of Schedule E - American Express Global Business Travel Divestiture

## Main Points Covered:

1. **Purpose and Scope**: Establishes Plan participation rules for employees of the proposed Global Business Travel joint venture who are entitled to benefits comparable to those offered by the Company.

2. **Applicability**: Schedule E governs "Business Employees" - individuals who were Plan participants and become employees of entities forming the joint venture business, with a requirement that the business documents obligate comparable benefit provision.

3. **Conflict Resolution**: In case of conflicts between Schedule E and other Plan provisions, Schedule E controls for Business Employees, though it does not affect previously accrued benefits.

4. **Eligibility Requirements** (Section 1.1):
   - Business Employees may continue participation during the period specified in business governing documents
   - Must have been Plan participants immediately before the business establishment or offered enrollment opportunity
   - Must enroll either before or after their Transition Date but within applicable deadlines
   - Administrator has discretion to exclude participants if Section 409A compliance is maintained

5. **Top-Hat Plan Status**: Participation is limited to select management or highly compensated employees to maintain "top-hat" plan qualification under ERISA.

6. **Termination of Participation** (Section 1.2): Participation ends upon the earlier of specified dates, including Separation from Service from the Business, with provisions for rehiring and resumption of participation.

nodes.title: Section 1.3 Change of Status
nodes.summary:
# Description of Partial Document

This section of a deferred compensation plan document covers the following main points:

1. **Termination of Participation**: Establishes that participation ends on the later of either when comparable benefits are no longer required under business documents or when Section 409A permits cessation of contributions.

2. **Change of Status**: Describes how individuals transitioning from Business Employee to Company Employee status cease participation in this plan (except for vested benefits from their Business Employee period), while preserving prior election validity.

3. **Deferral Benefits**: Details how participating Business Employees have compensation deducted from their Base Salary and Incentive Pay according to their filed elections, with specific definitions for how these terms apply to Business payments and rules for compensation aggregation under Section 401(a)(17) limitations.

4. **Election Rules**: Clarifies that prior Deferral Elections remain valid through status changes, but new elections cannot be filed after participation ceases (though employees may resume participation and file new elections if they again qualify).

5. **Supplemental Benefits**: Grants Amex discretionary authority to allocate supplemental benefits to Business Employees' accounts, including consideration of amounts paid by the Business.

6. **No Duplication**: Establishes a prohibition against providing duplicative benefits to any participant.

nodes.title: ARTICLE 2 CONTRIBUTIONS
nodes.summary:
# Description of Partial Document

This document excerpt covers provisions related to a deferred compensation plan for Business Employees, specifically addressing:

## Main Points:

1. **Termination of Participation** - Establishes the later of two dates for when participation ends: either when comparable benefits are no longer required under business documents, or when contribution cessation is permitted under Section 409A.

2. **Change of Status** - Describes how individuals transitioning from Business Employee to Company Employee status cease plan participation (except for vested benefits), while preserving prior elections.

3. **Contributions** - Details rules for:
   - Deferral Benefits deducted from Base Salary and Incentive Pay
   - Definitions of Base Salary and Incentive Pay for Business Employees
   - Compensation aggregation rules
   - Restrictions on filing new Deferral Elections after participation ceases
   - Supplemental Benefits allocation at company discretion
   - Prevention of duplicative benefits

4. **Funding** - Establishes that benefits remain unfunded obligations of the Company, with provisions allowing the Business to make payments while preserving the unfunded status and protecting creditor claims.

5. **Vesting** - Specifies that vesting is determined as if Business employment equals Company employment, with acceleration provisions aligned to the main RSP plan, except Company contributions follow the Business's 401(k) vesting schedule.

6. **Earnings and Payment** - Addresses account credits and payment timing/form, treating Business employment equivalent to Company employment for separation and rehire purposes.

nodes.title: Section 2.4 Funding of Benefits
nodes.summary:
# Description of Partial Document

This partial document excerpt covers key provisions of an employee benefit plan related to business operations and participant benefits:

## Main Points Covered:

1. **Funding of Benefits (Section 2.4)**: Benefits are unfunded obligations of the Company, with no preferential claim over general creditors. The Company may require the Business to make payments to a rabbi trust, but these remain subject to creditor claims to maintain the unfunded status.

2. **Vesting (Article 3)**: Vesting is determined as if employment with the Business equals employment with the Company. Vesting of contributions follows the Business's 401(k) plan schedule for Company contributions attributable to Business employment, without acceleration.

3. **Earnings (Article 4)**: Credits to participant accounts for benefits earned as a Business Employee are made according to Article 7 of the Plan.

4. **Payment of Benefits (Article 5)**:
   - **Normal Payment Terms (Section 5.1)**: Payment timing and form are determined as if Business employment equals Company employment. Participants do not experience a Separation from Service solely due to employment status change, unless required by Section 409A. Rehired participants follow standard rehire rules.
   - **Separation from Service (Section 5.2)**: Once a participant becomes a Business Employee, Separation from Service is determined by treating the Business and its affiliates as the "service recipient," with provisions for adjustments due to future transactions.

nodes.title: ARTICLE 3 VESTING
nodes.summary:
# Description of Partial Document

This partial document excerpt covers key provisions of an employee benefit plan related to funding, vesting, earnings, and payment of benefits for business employees:

**Main Points:**

1. **Funding of Benefits (Section 2.4):** Benefits are unfunded obligations of the Company, with no preferential claim over general creditors. The Company may require the Business to make payments to fund the plan or establish a rabbi trust, but such funds remain subject to creditor claims to maintain the unfunded status.

2. **Vesting (Article 3):** Vesting is determined as if employment with the Business equals employment with the Company. Vesting of contributions is accelerated to match RSP acceleration for Business Employees, except Company contributions attributable to Business employment follow the Business's 401(k) plan vesting schedule without acceleration.

3. **Earnings (Article 4):** Credits to participant accounts for benefits earned as a Business Employee are made according to Article 7 of the Plan.

4. **Payment of Benefits (Article 5):** 
   - Payment timing and form for Business Employees are determined as if they were Company employees
   - Employment with the Business does not constitute a Separation from Service unless required by Section 409A
   - Participants do not start a new participation period due to Business employment
   - Rehired participants follow normal rehire rules
   - Separation from Service is determined by treating the Business and its affiliates as the "service recipient" under tax code standards

4. **Payment of Benefits (Article 5)**:
   - **Normal Payment Terms (Section 5.1)**: Payment timing and form are determined as if the Business employment were Company employment. Participants do not experience a "Separation from Service" solely due to employment status change, unless required by Section 409A. Rehired participants follow standard rehire rules.
   - **Separation from Service (Section 5.2)**: Once a participant becomes a Business Employee, separation is determined by treating the Business and its affiliates as the "service recipient" under tax code standards, with provisions for adjustments based on future transactions.

nodes.title: ARTICLE 5 PAYMENT OF BENEFITS
nodes.summary:
# Description of Partial Document

This partial document is a Schedule E of an employee benefit plan that addresses the treatment of employees of an affiliated business entity ("Business Employees"). The main points covered include:

**Funding of Benefits (Section 2.4):**
- Plan benefits remain unfunded obligations of the Company
- Funds paid to the Company or rabbi trust remain subject to claims of general creditors
- No preferential payment rights are granted to participants

**Vesting (Article 3):**
- Business Employee vesting is determined as if employed by the Company
- Vesting of contributions accelerates consistent with the RSP (Retirement Savings Plan)
- Company contributions follow the Business's 401(k) plan vesting schedule without acceleration

**Earnings (Article 4):**
- Credits to participant accounts are made according to Article 7 of the Plan

**Payment of Benefits (Article 5):**
- Payment timing and form are determined as if employment were with the Company
- Separation from Service rules treat the Business as the service recipient for Section 409A compliance
- Beneficiary designations are unaffected by Business Employee status
- Rehire rules apply normally if a participant separates and is rehired

**Miscellaneous Provisions (Article 6):**
- Committee retains amendment and termination rights
- No superior payment rights or employment guarantees are created
- Administrator has authority to interpret and administer the Schedule in compliance with Section 409A

nodes.title: Section 5.1 Normal Time and Form of Payment
nodes.summary:
# Description of Partial Document

This partial document excerpt covers key provisions of an employee benefit plan related to funding, vesting, earnings, and payment of benefits for business employees:

**Main Points:**

1. **Funding of Benefits (Section 2.4):** Benefits are unfunded obligations of the Company, with no preferential claim over general creditors. The Company may require the Business to make payments to fund the plan or establish a rabbi trust, but these funds remain subject to creditor claims to maintain the unfunded status.

2. **Vesting (Article 3):** Vesting is determined as if employment with the Business equals employment with the Company. Vesting of contributions is accelerated to match RSP acceleration for Business Employees, except Company contributions attributable to Business employment follow the Business's 401(k) plan vesting schedule without acceleration.

3. **Earnings (Article 4):** Credits to Participant accounts for benefits earned as a Business Employee are made according to Article 7 of the Plan.

4. **Payment of Benefits (Article 5):** 
   - Payment timing and form for Business Employees are determined as if they were Company employees
   - Employment with the Business does not constitute a Separation from Service or new participation period
   - Separation from Service is determined by treating the Business and its affiliates as the "service recipient" under Section 414(b) or (c) of the Internal Revenue Code using the 80% common ownership standard

nodes.title: Section 5.2 Separation from Service
nodes.summary:
# Description of Partial Document

This partial document is a Schedule E of an employee benefit plan that addresses the treatment of employees of an affiliated business entity ("Business Employees"). The main points covered include:

**Funding of Benefits (Section 2.4):** Benefits remain unfunded obligations of the Company, with no preferential claims against the Business's assets ahead of general creditors, maintaining the plan's unfunded status.

**Vesting (Article 3):** Business Employee vesting is determined as if they were Company employees, with accelerated vesting matching the RSP plan, except Company contributions follow the Business's 401(k) vesting schedule without acceleration.

**Earnings (Article 4):** Credits to participant accounts for Business Employees are made according to Article 7 of the Plan.

**Payment of Benefits (Article 5):** 
- Payment timing and form are determined as if Business Employees were Company employees
- Separation from Service rules treat the Business and its affiliates as the service recipient
- Participants do not experience a Separation merely by switching between Business and Company employment
- Beneficiary designations remain unaffected by Business Employee status

**Miscellaneous (Article 6):** The Committee retains amendment and termination rights, the Schedule does not create superior benefit rights or employment guarantees, and the Administrator has authority to interpret and administer the Schedule in compliance with Section 409A.

nodes.title: Section 5.3 Beneficiaries
nodes.summary:
# Description of Partial Document

This partial document excerpt covers provisions related to Business Employees and plan administration within what appears to be an employee benefit plan schedule. The main points include:

1. **Reemployment Rules**: If a Business Employee is subsequently rehired as a regular Employee, their new benefits follow standard Separation from Service rules, while benefits from their prior Business Employee period remain governed by the special Business Employee provisions.

2. **Beneficiary Designations**: A participant's beneficiary designation remains valid and unaffected by a change in employment status to Business Employee.

3. **Committee Rights**: The Committee retains the authority to amend or terminate the Schedule or other plan provisions at any time without individual approval, subject to Section 409A compliance.

4. **Scope Limitations**: The Schedule does not grant Business Employees superior benefit rights compared to other participants, nor does it guarantee employment rights with the Company, Business, or affiliates.

5. **Administration and Interpretation**: The Administrator has authority to interpret, construe, and administer the Schedule consistent with the rest of the Plan, including handling claims through the normal claims process, with all interpretations required to comply with Section 409A.

The document emphasizes compliance with Section 409A (a tax code provision) throughout these provisions.

nodes.title: EXHIBIT 10.22 DESCRIPTION OF COMPENSATION PAYABLE TO NON-MANAGEMENT DIRECTORS
nodes.summary:
# Description of Non-Management Director Compensation at American Express Company

This document outlines the compensation structure approved by the Board of Directors of American Express Company on December 7, 2021, effective January 1, 2022, for non-management directors. The main points covered include:

**Base Compensation:**
- Annual retainer of $110,000 (subject to a $20,000 reduction if attendance falls below 75% of Board and committee meetings)

**Equity Compensation:**
- Grant of Share Equivalent Units valued at $220,000 awarded upon director election or reelection at the Annual Meeting of Shareholders

**Committee Leadership Fees:**
- $40,000 annual retainer for chairs of the Audit and Compliance Committee and Risk Committee
- $30,000 annual retainer for chairs of the Compensation and Benefits Committee and Nominating, Governance and Public Responsibility Committee

**Committee Member Fees:**
- $20,000 annual retainer for members of the Audit and Compliance Committee and Risk Committee
- $15,000 annual retainer for members of the Compensation and Benefits Committee and Nominating, Governance and Public Responsibility Committee

**Lead Director Compensation:**
- $100,000 annual fee comprised of $25,000 in cash and $75,000 in Share Equivalent Units

**Additional Benefits:**
- Reimbursement of customary expenses for attending Board, committee, and shareholder meetings

nodes.title: EXHIBIT 10.31 AMENDMENT TO RESTRICTED STOCK UNIT AWARD AGREEMENT (BAND 99) AND NONQUALIFIED STOCK OPTION AWARD AGREEMENT (BAND 99)
nodes.summary:
# Description of Exhibit 10.31

This document is an amendment to two Band 99 award agreements effective for awards granted on or after February 1, 2023.

## Key Points:

**Amendment to Restricted Stock Unit (RSU) Award Agreement:**
- **Death or Disability**: RSUs immediately vest with 100% payout upon participant's death or disability before the vesting date, removing other vesting conditions
- **Retirement**: 
  - Participants outside EU/UK: Awards continue and vest on the vesting date upon Early or Full Retirement if retirement occurs more than one year after grant date
  - Participants within EU/UK: Awards continue and vest on the vesting date upon EU/UK Retirement if retirement occurs more than one year after grant date
  - In both cases, vesting conditions are waived except for performance requirements
- **Payment**: Earned shares are issued at the specified time, less any shares withheld for tax obligations

**Amendment to Nonqualified Stock Option Award Agreement:**
- **Death or Disability**: Options immediately vest and become exercisable upon participant's death or disability before the vesting date, removing other vesting conditions
- **Retirement**:
  - Participants outside EU/UK: Options continue and vest on the vesting date upon Early or Full Retirement if retirement occurs more than one year after grant date
  - Participants within EU/UK: Options continue and vest on the vesting date upon EU/UK Retirement if retirement occurs more than one year after grant date
  - Performance requirements still apply; options are forfeited if retirement does not qualify for continuation

nodes.title: I. Amendment to Restricted Stock Unit Award Agreement (Band 99)
nodes.summary:
# Description of Exhibit 10.31

This document is an amendment to two Band 99 equity award agreements, effective for awards granted on or after February 1, 2023. The main points covered are:

## Amendment to Restricted Stock Unit Award Agreement (Band 99)

**Section 2 - Death or Disability:**
- Upon a participant's death (whether employed or retired), RSUs immediately vest at 100% payout
- Upon disability, RSUs immediately vest at 100% payout
- Vesting conditions are waived in both scenarios

**Section 3 - Retirement:**
- Distinguishes between participants outside the EU/UK versus those within the EU/UK
- For non-EU/UK participants: Awards continue and vest on the vesting date for both Early Retirement and Full Retirement (if retirement occurs more than one year after grant date)
- For EU/UK participants: Awards continue and vest on the vesting date for EU/UK Retirement (if retirement occurs more than one year after grant date)
- Employment vesting conditions are waived, but awards remain subject to performance conditions

**Section 6(c) - Payment:**
- Specifies that earned shares from retirement vesting are issued at the time specified in the agreement, less any shares withheld for tax obligations

## Amendment to Nonqualified Stock Option Award Agreement (Band 99)

**Section 3(a) - Death or Disability Vesting:**
- Upon death, options immediately vest and become exercisable
- Upon disability, options immediately vest and become exercisable
- Vesting conditions are waived in both scenarios
- Options may be exercised for all shares upon vesting

nodes.title: II. Amendment to Nonqualified Stock Option Award Agreement (Band 99)
nodes.summary:
# Description of Partial Document

This document outlines amendments to equity award agreements regarding vesting and payment provisions for participants, with specific focus on retirement, death, and disability scenarios.

## Main Points Covered:

1. **EU/UK Retirement Provisions for Restricted Stock Awards**: Participants with home base in the European Union or United Kingdom who retire (more than one year after grant date) will have their awards continue to vest on the scheduled vesting date, with earned shares determined under specified sections.

2. **Payment of Earned Shares Upon Retirement**: The Company will issue earned shares from vested awards to participants, minus any shares withheld for tax obligations, with timing specified in the agreement.

3. **Nonqualified Stock Option Award Amendments (Band 99)**: Effective February 1, 2023, amendments to Sections 3(a) and 4(a) of the stock option agreement are detailed.

4. **Death and Disability Provisions**: 
   - Options immediately vest upon participant death (whether before or after retirement)
   - Options immediately vest upon disability before the vesting date
   - Vested options become exercisable and are no longer subject to vesting conditions

5. **Retirement Vesting for Stock Options**:
   - **Non-EU/UK Participants**: Early retirement (>1 year after grant) and full retirement allow options to continue and vest on vesting date, subject to performance requirements
   - **EU/UK Participants**: EU/UK retirement (>1 year after grant) allows options to continue and vest on vesting date, subject to performance requirements

6. **Forfeiture Rules**: Options are forfeited and cancelled if retirement does not qualify under the specified conditions.

nodes.title: EXHIBIT 21 SUBSIDIARIES OF AMERICAN EXPRESS COMPANY
nodes.summary:
# Description of Exhibit 21: Subsidiaries of American Express Company

This document is a comprehensive list of subsidiaries owned by American Express Company as of December 31, 2022. The main points covered include:

1. **Scope and Structure**: Lists all voting securities of subsidiaries directly or indirectly owned by American Express Company, with indentation indicating principal parent-subsidiary relationships.

2. **Geographic Diversity**: Subsidiaries are organized across multiple countries and jurisdictions, including the United States, Europe (UK, Netherlands, Spain, Italy, France, Germany, Poland, Austria, Czech Republic, Sweden, Switzerland), Asia-Pacific (Japan, China, Hong Kong, Taiwan, Malaysia, Thailand, India, Indonesia, New Zealand, Australia), Americas (Canada, Mexico, Argentina, Chile, Brazil), and Middle East (Saudi Arabia, Bahrain, Oman, Qatar, Egypt).

3. **Business Operations**: Subsidiaries cover various business segments including:
   - Banking and credit services
   - Travel-related services
   - Payment processing and services
   - Insurance services
   - Innovation and technology labs
   - Loyalty programs (PAYBACK, Loyalty Partner)
   - Receivables financing corporations
   - Specialized services (Resy Network, LoungeBuddy, Pocket Concierge)

4. **Ownership Notes**: Asterisks (*) indicate subsidiaries where American Express does not own all voting securities, including entities in the Middle East and one in China.

5. **Organizational Hierarchy**: The document reflects the complex corporate structure with multiple holding companies and intermediate entities managing regional and functional operations.

nodes.title: EXHIBIT 31.1 CERTIFICATION
nodes.summary:
# Description of Exhibit 31.1 - CEO Certification

This document is a Section 302 certification required under the Sarbanes-Oxley Act, signed by Stephen J. Squeri, Chief Executive Officer of American Express Company, dated February 10, 2023.

## Main Points Covered:

1. **Report Review and Accuracy**: The CEO certifies that he has reviewed the Form 10-K annual report and confirms it contains no untrue or misleading material statements.

2. **Financial Statement Fairness**: The CEO attests that the financial statements and financial information in the report fairly present the company's financial condition, results of operations, and cash flows for the periods covered.

3. **Disclosure Controls and Procedures**: The CEO confirms responsibility for establishing and maintaining disclosure controls designed to ensure material information is communicated to management during report preparation.

4. **Internal Control Over Financial Reporting**: The CEO certifies responsibility for internal controls designed to provide reasonable assurance regarding the reliability of financial reporting and compliance with generally accepted accounting principles (GAAP).

5. **Effectiveness Evaluation**: The CEO reports conclusions regarding the effectiveness of disclosure controls and procedures as of the report period end.

6. **Changes in Internal Controls**: The CEO discloses any material changes in internal control over financial reporting during the most recent fiscal quarter.

7. **Disclosure of Control Deficiencies**: The CEO confirms disclosure to auditors and the audit committee of any significant deficiencies, material weaknesses, or fraud involving management or employees with significant roles in internal controls.

nodes.title: EXHIBIT 31.2 CERTIFICATION
nodes.summary:
# Description of Exhibit 31.2 - Certification

This document is a Section 302 certification required under the Sarbanes-Oxley Act, signed by Jeffrey C. Campbell, Chief Financial Officer of American Express Company, dated February 10, 2023.

## Main Points Covered:

1. **Report Review and Accuracy**: The CFO certifies that he has reviewed the Form 10-K annual report and confirms it contains no untrue or misleading material statements.

2. **Financial Statement Fairness**: The CFO attests that the financial statements and financial information in the report fairly present the company's financial condition, results of operations, and cash flows for the periods covered.

3. **Disclosure Controls and Procedures**: The CFO confirms responsibility for establishing and maintaining disclosure controls designed to ensure material information is communicated to management during report preparation.

4. **Internal Control Over Financial Reporting**: The CFO certifies responsibility for internal controls designed to provide reasonable assurance regarding the reliability of financial reporting and compliance with generally accepted accounting principles (GAAP).

5. **Effectiveness Evaluation**: The CFO confirms that disclosure controls and procedures have been evaluated and their effectiveness assessed as of the report period's end.

6. **Changes in Internal Controls**: The CFO discloses any material changes in internal control over financial reporting that occurred during the most recent fiscal quarter.

7. **Disclosure of Control Deficiencies and Fraud**: The CFO confirms disclosure to auditors and the audit committee of all significant deficiencies, material weaknesses, and any fraud involving management or employees with significant roles in internal controls.

nodes.title: EXHIBIT 32.1 Certification Pursuant to 18 U.S.C. Section 1350, as Adopted Pursuant to Section 906 of the Sarbanes-Oxley Act of 2002
nodes.summary:
# Description of Exhibit 32.1

This document is a CEO certification required under Section 906 of the Sarbanes-Oxley Act of 2002. The main points covered include:

1. **Certification Authority**: Stephen J. Squeri, Chief Executive Officer of American Express Company, certifies the company's Annual Report on Form 10-K for the fiscal year ended December 31, 2022, pursuant to 18 U.S.C. Section 1350.

2. **Compliance Statement**: The CEO certifies that the Report fully complies with the requirements of Section 13(a) or 15(d) of the Securities Exchange Act of 1934.

3. **Financial Accuracy**: The CEO certifies that the information contained in the Report fairly presents, in all material respects, the financial condition and results of operations of American Express Company.

4. **Legal Disclaimers**: The certification clarifies that it is furnished solely under Section 906 of the Sarbanes-Oxley Act and is not filed as part of the Form 10-K or subject to liability under Section 18 of the Securities Exchange Act of 1934. It also states that the certification will not be incorporated by reference into other filings unless expressly specified.

5. **Document Retention**: A signed original of the certification has been provided to the Company and will be retained and furnished to the SEC upon request.

nodes.title: EXHIBIT 32.2 Certification Pursuant to 18 U.S.C. Section 1350, as Adopted Pursuant to Section 906 of the Sarbanes-Oxley Act of 2002
nodes.summary:
# Description of Exhibit 32.2

This document is a Section 906 certification required under the Sarbanes-Oxley Act of 2002. The main points covered include:

1. **Certifying Officer**: Jeffrey C. Campbell, Chief Financial Officer of American Express Company, provides the certification for the company's Form 10-K Annual Report for the fiscal year ended December 31, 2022.

2. **Compliance Certification**: The CFO certifies that the Report fully complies with the requirements of Section 13(a) or 15(d) of the Securities Exchange Act of 1934.

3. **Financial Accuracy Certification**: The CFO certifies that the financial information contained in the Report fairly presents, in all material respects, the financial condition and results of operations of the Company.

4. **Legal Basis**: The certification is made pursuant to 18 U.S.C. Section 1350, as adopted under Section 906 of the Sarbanes-Oxley Act of 2002.

5. **Filing Status and Liability Disclaimer**: The document clarifies that this certification is furnished solely under Section 1350 and is not filed as part of the Form 10-K or as a separate disclosure document, and therefore is not subject to liability under Section 18 of the Securities Exchange Act of 1934.

6. **Document Retention**: A signed original of the certification has been provided to the Company and will be retained and furnished to the SEC upon request.
